rows.notes: Page title clearly written at top centre: 'Recepta. Anni presentis 1775'. Very legible script and no amounts adjacent.

rows.description: South Newington Rect: pro dim: An: ad f. Annunc:
rows.notes: Amounts aligned in the pounds/shillings/pence columns as '28 0 0'. Numerals are clear and pass validation (shillings 0, pence 0).
rows.english_description: Half-yearly payment from the South Newington rectory, due at the Annunciation (Lady Day).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Half-yearly payment from the South Newington rectory, due at the Annunciation, amounting to £28 0s 0d.

rows.notes: Clear numeric trio '30 0 0' in the three right-hand columns. Description reasonably legible as 'Guinear Rect. ad fest: Annunc:'.
rows.english_description: Payment from the Guinear rectory received at the Annunciation (Lady Day), representing the half-yearly portion.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment from the Guinear rectory at the Annunciation, amounting to £30 0s 0d.

rows.notes: Pounds/shillings read as '85 4'. Pence reads as '1' with a small loop/mark interpreted as '3q' (three farthings) → 0.75. Column alignment is consistent; fraction interpretation is plausible though the tiny mark is slightly stylized.
rows.english_description: Rent received from the Long Wittenham rectory.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Rent received from the Long Wittenham rectory, amounting to £85 4s 1 3/4d.

rows.notes: Pounds appear as three-digit '114' (the leading '1' is narrow but present). Shillings '8' and pence '0' are clear. Three-digit pounds are legible though the leading '1' is slightly faint.
rows.english_description: Augmentation payment for Kidlington payable at Candlemas (an ecclesiastical augmentation/top-up).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Augmentation payment for Kidlington at Candlemas, amounting to £114 8s 0d.

rows.notes: Indented sub-item under previous header; amounts read clearly as '02 15 0'. Description fairly clear ('Capons and Brown').
rows.english_description: Receipt of capons and brown (provisions) received by the college.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Receipt of capons and brown (provisions), amounting to £2 15s 0d.

rows.notes: Amounts read '20 0 0' clearly. Handwriting of place-name is slightly idiosyncratic ('Minchinnet') but readable in context as a vicarage entry.
rows.english_description: Payment from the vicarage of Minchinnet.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment from the vicarage of Minchinnet, amounting to £20 0s 0d.

rows.notes: Pounds read as '84' (the '8' and '4' are clear), shillings and pence '0 0'. The place name looks like 'Hidlington' (handwriting could be 'Kidlington' variant) — description transcribed as it appears.
rows.english_description: Payment from the vicarage of Hidlington.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment from the vicarage of Hidlington, amounting to £84 0s 0d.

rows.notes: Clear '10 0 0' in amount columns. Description 'Yardington Rect:' legible.
rows.english_description: Rent received from the Yardington rectory.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Rent received from the Yardington rectory, amounting to £10 0s 0d.

rows.notes: Amounts read '08 14 5½' — pence shows '5' with a clear 'ob' / loop indicating half-penny → 0.5. Description is abbreviated ('Ten m') and slightly uncertain due to abbreviation; numeric columns are well aligned but the abbreviation reduces certainty.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Rent from a tenement, amounting to £8 14s 5 1/2d.

rows.notes: Amounts '02 10 0' are clear. Description reads 'Thrup Annuity & L Day' though first word could be 'Thrup' or 'Thorp' — numerals unaffected.
rows.english_description: Annual annuity from Thrup (paid at Lady Day).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Annual annuity from Thrup, paid at Lady Day, amounting to £2 10s 0d.

rows.notes: Amounts read '11 8 1¾' — pence '1' with a '3q' style mark (three farthings) interpreted as 0.75. Description 'Kidlington Mills (in Part)' is legible; fraction mark is small but consistent with other 3-quarter marks on page.
rows.english_description: Part payment from the Kidlington mills (a portion of mill rents).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Partial payment from the Kidlington mills, amounting to £11 8s 1 3/4d.

rows.description: Bensington pro dim: an: ad T. Annunc:
rows.notes: Amounts read '02 16 9¾' — pence '9' plus a small '3q' mark indicating three farthings (0.75). The description is heavily abbreviated; reading chosen to match visible abbreviations. Fraction mark is faint but consistent with other '¾' marks.
rows.english_description: Half-yearly payment from Bensington due at the Annunciation (Lady Day).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Half-yearly payment from Bensington at the Annunciation, amounting to £2 16s 9 3/4d.

rows.notes: Amounts '03 6 6' are clear. Place/name reading 'Tistinhull, Napper' is plausible from script; abbreviation 'pro dicto temp:' follows and is readable. No fraction mark present.
rows.english_description: Payment from Tistinhull and Napper for the stated period (payments from named tenants).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment from Tistinhull and Napper for the stated period, amounting to £3 6s 6d.

rows.notes: Amounts read '01 13 4' clearly. Description 'Hertford Coll: olim Hart Hall' is legible and matches typical Latin phrasing (olim = formerly).
rows.english_description: Payment received from Hertford College (formerly Hart Hall).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment received from Hertford College (formerly Hart Hall), amounting to £1 13s 4d.

rows.description: Trathewan pro dim: An. ad T. Annunc:
rows.notes: Amounts '03 0 0' are clear. Place-name 'Trathewan' (or similar) is somewhat stylized but the abbreviation and amount alignment make the entry unambiguous as an income line.
rows.english_description: Half-yearly payment from Trathewan due at the Annunciation (Lady Day).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Half-yearly payment from Trathewan at the Annunciation, amounting to £3 0s 0d.

rows.notes: Total line reads '327 10 4' clearly in the three numeric columns. There is a small additional mark to the right of the pence that might be a flourish or an extra fraction marker; it is ambiguous and was not interpreted as a fractional farthing because the shape is unclear. NOTE: individual row sums as transcribed above do not arithmetically sum to this total without making assumptions about ambiguous place-name numerals (several pounds-values are stylistically similar and could admit alternate readings). Chosen total is the printed total on the page and transcribed as-is.
